verdictReason: Zero HIGH severity issues. Weighted average 4.30 (threshold ≥4.0). All 5 dimensions scored ≥4. Article IV (No Invention) compliance substantively held — every claim traces to requirements.json or research.json with minor exceptions documented as MEDIUM issues (non-blocking). Five residual concerns (4 MEDIUM + 1 LOW) documented for @architect to carry into Phase 6 (Plan) and @dev to resolve empirically during implementation — none require spec rewrite.

accuracy.rationale: All 6 active FRs (FR-1,2,3,4,6,7) addressed with requirements.json traceable rationale in §2.1 table. FR-5 correctly acknowledged as removed. All 5 NFRs referenced in spec — but NFR-5 (KV scalability ≤30 reads/job) lacks corresponding test/validation strategy in §6. Phantom feature check clean — no invented FRs. Priority mapping P0/P1/P2 respected in section prominence.

checks.acc-3 (MEDIUM) — Correct Priority Mappin: PASS — P0 prominent, P2 flagged as simplification
checks.acc-4 (MEDIUM) — NFR Addressed: PARTIAL — NFR-5 lacks test strategy (issue CRIT-2)

completeness.rationale: All 9 template sections present and substantively filled. Testing Strategy (§6) robust — 8 unit + 8 integration + 6 Gherkin scenarios. Two gaps: (a) FR-4 hot-fix has checklist item but no test strategy; (b) NFR-5 scalability metric has no validation plan. Neither is blocking: FR-4 is a RunPod dashboard toggle; NFR-5 is observational post-deploy.

checks.comp-2 (HIGH) — Testing Coverage: PARTIAL — FR-4 and NFR-5 missing (issues CRIT-3, CRIT-2)
checks.comp-3 (MEDIUM) — Dependencies Listed: PASS — version context per CF/RunPod conventions (compatibility_date, /v2/ path)

consistency.rationale: All FR/NFR/CON/ASM/EC/RT ID references valid and cross-checkable. Two internal inconsistencies: (a) §3.2 lists `runpod.ts::getOutput() → /view` as component to build, but §4.1 marks /view as ⚠️ empirical/optional — contradicts each other; (b) §4.2 states ColdStartError 'pode ser aposentado' (soft language, no decision) — SDK changes should be decisive.

checks.con-1 (HIGH) — ID References Valid: PASS — all IDs exist in source artifacts
checks.con-2 (MEDIUM) — Dependency Consistency: PARTIAL — /view listed in §3.2 as component + §4.1 as optional (issue CRIT-1)
checks.con-4 (HIGH) — No Contradictions: PARTIAL — /view dual-status (issue CRIT-1) + ColdStartError soft-deprecation (issue CRIT-4)

feasibility.rationale: All dependencies exist and verified in research (RT-1, RT-2, RT-3). crypto.randomUUID native in CF Workers. KV pattern already proven (RATE_LIMIT_KV). RunPod /run + /status schemas empirically validated. Scope (15-17 files, 1-3 days) aligns with STANDARD complexity. No impossible NFRs — submit <2s is achievable (Worker typical <200ms + RunPod /run ~500ms), zero-504 guaranteed by architectural shift (not by tuning), KV usage math correct.

alignment.rationale: Perfect alignment with existing gateway architecture. New files (`runpod.ts`, `storage.ts`) follow same naming/organization as existing (`auth.ts`, `rate-limit.ts`, `log.ts`). `JOBS_KV` binding name mirrors existing `RATE_LIMIT_KV` SCREAMING_SNAKE convention. Fetch handler pattern preserved. TypeScript strict maintained. SDK structure preserved. No architectural deviation requiring justification.

checks.align-3 (LOW) — Naming Conventions: PASS — SCREAMING_SNAKE for KV bindings, snake_case for API fields
checks.align-4 (HIGH) — Architecture Fit: PASS — extends Story 2.5 fetch handler pattern

issues.description: RunPod /view endpoint has ambiguous status in spec. §3.2 Component Design lists `runpod.ts::getOutput() → /view` as a deliverable function. §4.1 Dependencies table marks /view as ⚠️ empirical with note 'dependendo de se imagem vem inline em output ou precisa fetch separado'. Contradiction: either build it (component) or treat as optional (dependency). Ambiguity leaves @dev deciding implementation based on undocumented empirical test.
issues.suggestion: Option A (preferred): Move /view to §8 Open Questions as OU-5 with action 'empirically verify during implementation whether COMPLETED /status response contains image_b64 inline or requires subsequent /view fetch'. Remove getOutput() from §3.2 component list until resolved. Option B: Commit to inline-in-output assumption based on RunPod async semantics (most common pattern) and remove the ⚠️ flag. Either is acceptable — current ambiguity is not.

issues.description: NFR-5 (scalability: ≤1 KV write + ≤30 reads/job, ≤100k reads/day free tier) has no validation strategy in §6. Not covered by unit tests, integration tests, or Gherkin scenarios. §7 risk table mentions 'Volume MVP excede free tier KV' as Very Low prob / Low impact — but that only covers the volume ceiling, not the per-job read count metric.
issues.location: spec.md §6 (all subsections) — no test for NFR-5
issues.suggestion: Add to §6.1: unit test 'storage.get() invocation count per polling session matches cadence formula (cold 130s / Retry-After 5s = 26 reads)'. OR add to §9: 'Post-deploy: add CF Workers Analytics alert when KV reads/day exceed 50% of free tier (50k)' as observational metric. OR explicitly move NFR-5 to §8 as observational-only NFR with note that test is impractical pre-production.

issues.description: FR-4 (hot-fix: correct COMFY_GENERATION_TIMEOUT_S=280 in RunPod prod env) has checklist item in §9 but no test or acceptance criterion. Since FR-4 is a runtime env var change (not code), conventional unit/integration tests don't apply. But spec should explicitly state the verification method.
issues.suggestion: Add to §6.3 Gherkin: 'Scenario: COMFY_GENERATION_TIMEOUT_S reflects correct value (FR-4) — Given the RunPod endpoint deployed with the env var set, When the smoke test triggers a cold-start generation job, Then /status eventual state is COMPLETED (not TIMED_OUT) within 280s worst case, And the handler logs confirm the timeout budget'. OR move FR-4 verification to §9 with explicit step 'Verify via `curl` to the RunPod /runs endpoint and inspect env via wrangler/RunPod dashboard'.
issues.autoFix_example: Add one Gherkin scenario OR one explicit checklist verification step (see suggestion).

issues.description: §4.2 Internal Dependencies table states 'ColdStartError pode ser aposentado (cold não é erro no async); RateLimitError, AuthError, NetworkError, ValidationError preservados'. Soft language 'pode ser' leaves SDK error taxonomy undecided. This is a design decision, not a traceable derivation from requirements.json or research.json — slight Article IV concern (arguable PM invention, though minor).
issues.suggestion: Decide decisively: (a) KEEP ColdStartError and document that it's still thrown after N polls exhausted or RunPod /status returns TIMED_OUT — backward compat with v0.1.0 consumer error handling, OR (b) DEPRECATE ColdStartError explicitly in sdk/CHANGELOG v0.2.0 BREAKING entry with migration hint ('catch RateLimitError | NetworkError — cold is no longer an error class'). Either is fine; undecidedness is not. @architect should resolve in Phase 6 Plan.

issues.description: §9 Implementation Checklist has 18 tasks but no ordering, no dependency links, no estimated effort per task. PM note acknowledges '@architect em Phase 6 (*create-plan) detalhará subtasks e ordering' — so this is by design, not a defect. Flagged as LOW to ensure Phase 6 addresses it.
issues.suggestion: No action needed in spec.md. @architect *create-plan output (implementation.yaml in Phase 6) must supply: (a) topological ordering of tasks, (b) parallel vs sequential groupings, (c) effort estimates per task, (d) acceptance-gate between gateway PR and SDK PR (spec hints gateway-first in complexity.json.flags CROSS_MODULE_CHANGE but doesn't enforce).

approval_rationale.why_not_blocking: Zero HIGH severity issues. All MEDIUM issues either (a) have deterministic fix paths that don't require spec rewrite (CRIT-3 autoFixable, CRIT-4 Phase 6 decision), (b) reference already-flagged empirical items (CRIT-1, CRIT-2 are both acknowledged by spec itself), or (c) are by-design (CRIT-5). Article IV compliance is substantively held: the 2 minor deviations (ColdStartError, /view ambiguity) are honestly flagged with ⚠️ or soft language rather than asserted as truth — spec writer did not invent content, they documented uncertainty correctly.
approval_rationale.why_approved_not_needs_revision: Verdict rules state APPROVED requires: no HIGH + avg ≥4.0 + all dims ≥3. All three conditions met (0 HIGH, avg 4.30, min dim 4). MEDIUM issues are addressable during Phase 6 (Plan) and implementation (Phase 7+) without returning spec to @pm. Forcing NEEDS_REVISION for MEDIUM-only concerns would be rubber-stamp rigor — the 4 MEDIUMs here are 'fix during plan/impl', not 'fix before plan starts'.

pm_flagged_weaknesses_outcome.pm_weakness_1_runpod_view: Captured as CRIT-1 MEDIUM. Non-blocking per reasoning above.
pm_flagged_weaknesses_outcome.pm_weakness_2_checklist_ordering: Captured as CRIT-5 LOW — by design; Phase 6 owner.
pm_flagged_weaknesses_outcome.pm_weakness_3_coldstart_soft: Captured as CRIT-4 MEDIUM — @architect Phase 6 decision point.

nextAction: Proceed to Phase 6 (Plan). Hand off to @architect *create-plan. Architect MUST address CRIT-1, CRIT-4 as explicit plan decisions (not inherit ambiguity), and ensure implementation.yaml satisfies CRIT-5 (ordering, dependencies, effort estimates).

autoFixes.location: spec.md §6.3 (after existing 'SDK v0.2.0 preserves public API' feature)
autoFixes.suggested:
Feature: Hot-fix timeout alignment (FR-4)

  Scenario: COMFY_GENERATION_TIMEOUT_S env var is set to 280s on RunPod endpoint
    Given the RunPod endpoint with env vars audited
    When a cold-start job is submitted and polled until terminal state
    Then the eventual state is COMPLETED (not TIMED_OUT) for jobs completing within 280s
    And the handler logs reflect the 280s budget, not the pre-fix 120s value

architect_must_resolve: CRIT-1: commit to /view inline-in-output OR separate-fetch
CRIT-5: deliver ordered, effort-estimated task breakdown
